interactions.user_query: Show me the full content of article ID 4ahkqy7d4g30k6k569n3svaxxs.
interactions.final_answer:
### Article: FY2024 Results: $638B Revenue, AWS Reaches $108B — What the Numbers Mean for Amazonians - **ID**: `4ahkqy7d4g30k6k569n3svaxxs` - **Channel**: The Daily (ID: `411t3m6z6cdev4nnbjjarkaf5k`) - **Tags**: `fy2024`, `earnings`, `aws`, `financial-results` - **Published**: 2026-04-28T04:35:11Z - **Image**: None - **Flags**: All standard (likes/comments enabled, no read confirmation, auto-related on) **Full Body (HTML)**: ```

Amazon Closes FY2024 with $638 Billion in Revenue

Amazon.com, Inc. reported full-year FY2024 revenue of $638 billion, marking another year of growth across all major business segments. For Amazonians at every level of the organisation, these results reflect the cumulative output of 1.5 million employees working across fulfilment centres, engineering offices, data centres, and delivery routes on six continents.

This document summarises the key figures, segment performance, and what leadership has communicated about the path forward.

Segment Performance: Where the Growth Came From

North America Retail

North America Retail remains the largest segment by revenue, contributing approximately $387 billion. This includes Amazon.com, Amazon Fresh, Amazon Go cashierless stores, and the Whole Foods Market chain (~500 stores, acquired 2017). Margins in retail remain deliberately thin — the business model prioritises volume, Prime membership growth, and long-term data advantage over short-term margin expansion.

International Retail

International Retail contributed approximately $143 billion across 20+ country storefronts including the UK, Germany, Japan, India, Australia, and Saudi Arabia. Most international segments continue to operate at a loss while building infrastructure. India remains a long-term strategic priority — Amazon has invested over $15 billion in Amazon.in to date.

Amazon Web Services

AWS delivered $108 billion in revenue at an operating margin of approximately 37%. This is not a footnote — AWS is where the overwhelming majority of Amazon's operating profit originates. Without AWS, the consolidated retail business would be marginally profitable at best. The segment's growth is being accelerated by enterprise AI demand, with Amazon Bedrock and Amazon Q driving new workload migrations to AWS infrastructure.

Advertising Services

Amazon's advertising business — now the third-largest digital advertising platform globally behind Google and Meta — contributed approximately $56 billion. This segment is high-margin and often underestimated by external analysts. Sponsored products, display ads, and streaming video advertising across Prime Video are the primary drivers.

Subscriptions (Prime)

Amazon Prime membership fees and digital content subscriptions generated approximately $44 billion. With over 200 million Prime members globally, Prime remains the structural glue of the retail flywheel — driving higher purchase frequency, larger basket sizes, and deeper ecosystem engagement across delivery, video, music, and reading.

What Leadership Has Said

"The results reflect what happens when you combine operational discipline with long-term investment thinking. AWS is growing faster than we projected two years ago, and the advertising business has become a genuine profit engine. We are not done." — Andy Jassy, CEO

What This Means for Amazonians

  • Operational employees: Volume commitments for 2025 are set based on these results. Expect continued investment in automation and last-mile infrastructure.
  • AWS employees: The $108B milestone is the baseline. Leadership has communicated that AI-driven workloads represent the next growth vector — expect headcount and infrastructure investment to follow.
  • Corporate employees: RSU vesting schedules are tied to stock performance. AMZN is listed on NASDAQ — full investor materials are available at ir.aboutamazon.com.
  • All employees: The FY2024 Sustainability Report, publishing alongside earnings, includes updates on Climate Pledge progress, renewable energy capacity, and packaging reduction milestones.

Full Earnings Materials

The complete earnings release, financial tables, and CEO shareholder letter are available at ir.aboutamazon.com. The investor call transcript will be posted within 48 hours of the earnings event.

Questions from employees about the financial results should be directed to your manager or raised at the next All-Hands. Do not share non-public financial information externally.

``` What next (e.g., update this article, view another, create new)?
